projected_roadmap.title: Automated Marketing-to-Sales ROI Dashboard
projected_roadmap.what: Build a live Looker Studio or CRM-integrated dashboard that connects Paid Ads spend directly to closed revenue from your CRM. This eliminates manual cross-referencing of call reports.
projected_roadmap.why_now: Currently, Ryan is manually cross-referencing reports for ROI tracking (as seen in recent tasks). With revenue hitting $280K/week, manual tracking is prone to error and consumes too much management time.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 8-10 hours to build connections and visualize data; 1 hour monthly for maintenance.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Grant API access to CRM and provide 30 minutes to review data accuracy for the first week.
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Complete 'Cross-reference call lead reports' to verify manual baseline.
projected_roadmap.impact: Provides real-time visibility into which campaigns (AC vs. Plumbing) are actually driving bankable revenue, not just leads.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Ryan, you've been doing a ton of heavy lifting manually cross-referencing these call reports to find our ROI. Now that we're scaling to $280K weeks, I want to take that off your plate. If we build out an automated ROI dashboard, you'll be able to see exactly where every dollar is going in real-time without the spreadsheet headache. What do you think about us setting that up this week?

projected_roadmap.title: Local Service Ads (LSA) Review & 'Messaging' Toggle Optimization
projected_roadmap.what: A deep-dive audit of the LSA account to disable the 'messaging' feature as discussed in the last meeting, and optimize the 'Business Bio' for the new plumbing services.
projected_roadmap.why_now: The March 24th meeting summary explicitly mentioned potentially disabling LSA messaging to improve lead quality. This is a direct follow-up to that strategic decision.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 2 hours to audit settings, toggle off messaging, and update plumbing service categories.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Approval to disable messaging feature (aware that lead volume might dip slightly but quality will rise).
projected_roadmap.impact: Reduces the 'junk' lead volume that the intake team currently has to filter through, focusing their time on high-intent phone calls.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: We talked last time about how those LSA messages can sometimes be more noise than signal. I'd like to go ahead and toggle that off and give the whole LSA profile a tune-up specifically for the new plumbing launch. It’ll ensure your team is only answering high-quality calls instead of chasing tire-kickers in the chat.

projected_roadmap.title: Service Area 'Hyper-Local' SEO Landing Pages
projected_roadmap.what: Creation of three dedicated SEO landing pages for Elk Grove, Modesto, and Galt. These pages will feature location-specific testimonials and service maps to boost organic rankings alongside our paid ads.
projected_roadmap.why_now: We just successfully implemented the geographic expansion in Paid Ads. To maximize our presence in these new markets and lower our blended Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), we need organic authority to match our ad spend.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 4 hours per page (12 hours total) for copy, design, and SEO metadata setup.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Provide 3-5 photos of recent jobs or happy customers in those specific areas.
projected_roadmap.impact: Increases organic lead flow from new territories, reducing the long-term reliance on high-cost PPC clicks in competitive HVAC markets.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Now that we're seeing good traction with the ads in Elk Grove and Modesto, I want to make sure we're not just 'renting' space there. If we build out some hyper-local pages for those areas, we can start winning those organic clicks too, which is going to bring our overall cost per lead down significantly as we head into the summer rush.

talking_points.Launch approved AC Ads headlines: Great news—we've got those new AC headlines live, just in time for the temperature shift we're seeing.
talking_points.Implement geographic expansion and budge: The expansion into Elk Grove and Modesto is officially rolled out and the budget is scaling as we planned.
talking_points.Notify intake team of new service areas: Ryan, quick check-in: has the intake team been briefed on the new service areas? We want to make sure they're ready to handle those Elk Grove calls coming in.
talking_points.Finalize AC promotional offers with Hayd: I want to make sure we don't lose momentum on the AC offers we discussed with Hayden—should we keep the current promotion or do you want to pivot for April?
talking_points.Update email copy to remove 'flush' verb: I'm still holding on that email copy update to remove the 'flush' wording—do we have the final replacement phrasing you'd like to use?
talking_points.Setup tracking number for plumbing campa: Ryan Watson, do you have a timeline for that plumbing tracking number? We're ready to flip the switch on the landing page as soon as that's in place.

If we build out an automated ROI dashboard, you'll be able to see exactly where every dollar is going in real-time without the spreadsheet headache. What do you think?
talking_points.Local Service Ads (LSA) Review & 'Messag: We talked last time about how those LSA messages can sometimes be more noise than signal. I'd like to go ahead and toggle that off and give the profile a tune-up for the plumbing launch.
